portfolio.name: Lee Seoung Hyun
portfolio.intro: I am a developer who constantly thinks about efficiency. I automate repetitive tasks and simplify complex structures. Unable to end the day without solving problems, my mind continues to asynchronously compute solutions even after work.

codeit.title: Codeit Sprint FE Bootcamp
codeit.description:
Intensively studied frontend development for 6 months at Codeit Sprint FE Bootcamp.
Developed collaboration experience and problem-solving skills through real projects using various tools and technologies.

data.growthJourney:
- During high school, I focused on learning while contemplating my career path, cultivating consistency and self-direction.
- In university, I experienced the joy of communication and knowledge sharing through presentation activities.
- At a VR theme park, I observed how planning and programs impact user experience in the field, sparking my interest in user-centered development and starting my frontend development journey.
- Through a bootcamp, I learned and applied frontend technologies while developing problem-solving skills.
- In professional work, I'm growing both my technical and communication capabilities.

details: Reduced test execution time by 67.7% relative to test count through parallel test processing using Dynamic metrics and Playwright Sharding
Fully migrated existing ESLint + Prettier-based lint automation to Biome, reducing average CI time by 98% and improving overall development speed
Developed custom onboarding guide component and improved personalized copy, achieving 42% increase in new user payment calendar entry rate and 17% increase in mobile revisit rate
Improved search efficiency through structured metadata addition and streamlined management through internal documentation
Identified missing metadata issues per page and abstracted into common logic to streamline management
Improved responsive image component with higher quality images, reducing CLS from 0.99 to 0.1 and LCP from 1.62 to 0.55
Actively used the performance tab to precisely identify areas requiring memoization and resolved unnecessary flickering
Documented issues with the existing server action-based communication architecture and established team-wide API architecture standards

Introduced MSW to improve mock data management efficiency and simplify asynchronous communication handling
Migrated CRA-based project to Vite, eliminating security risks
Replaced editor with react-quill-new and custom module react-quill-resize to address internal CSP policy and ensure image upload stability
Reduced maximum bundle size from 1200kB to 500kB (58.3% reduction) through Rollup optimization
Improved structural stability and predictability by separating server state and UI state that were previously coupled in zustand
Enhanced maintainability efficiency through feature-based folder structure transition and documentation
Implemented and optimized custom PDF Viewer using Canvas API
Reduced initial rendering time by 67% (5401ms → 1780ms) through Web Worker, react-window, and code splitting
Migrated CRA-based project to Vite, resolving CSP and performance issues
Reduced Docker image size from 2.22GB to 195MB (91.36% reduction) by applying multi-stage build and Standalone mode
Reduced bundle size from 302kB to 143kB (73.5% reduction) by removing unnecessary modules and using dynamic import

aboutMe.title: About Me
paragraphs: I enjoy [B]reading library source code and browser internals[/B] directly. From analyzing Chrome CPP to understand autocomplete behavior, to documenting Server Action's structural limits to kick off a team-wide tRPC migration — I tend not to leave uncertain things unverified.

I see developer productivity as a [B]team-level problem[/B]. I've cut test time 67.7% with Playwright parallelization, reduced CI lint time 98% with Biome, and built a Claude Code hooks-based macOS widget distributed via Homebrew — I enjoy designing the tools themselves.

skills.title: Skills
items: Primarily develops services on [B]Next.js App Router[/B]. Applies Server Components and Server Actions in production, and covers the broader Next.js ecosystem including type-safe API layers with tRPC and [B]Schema.org JSON-LD structured data[/B] design.

Uses TypeScript as a [B]design tool[/B], not just annotation. Eliminates impossible states at compile time with Discriminated Unions, shares schemas across server/client boundaries with Zod, and regularly reads library source to understand internals.

Designs test environments from scratch. Built parallel CI with [B]Playwright Dynamic metric and Sharding[/B] ([B]67.7% faster tests[/B]), designed domain-separated MSW handlers for 6+ domains, and replaced ESLint+Prettier with [B]Biome[/B] for [B]98% faster lint[/B].

Optimizes both build-time and runtime performance. [B]58.3% bundle reduction[/B] via Vite migration, [B]91.36% Docker image reduction[/B] via multi-stage builds, [B]67% render improvement[/B] via Web Worker and virtualization — always validates with numbers.

Has a strong interest in browser internals and security. Analyzed [B]Chrome CPP source[/B] to understand autocomplete behavior, replaced a CSP-violating editor with a custom module, and contributed to [B]open source[/B] after finding a bug in OpenReplay.

allra.summary: Series B fintech startup providing early payment (선정산) services to online marketplace sellers. Improved developer productivity through [B]test and CI pipeline enhancements[/B], alongside SEO improvements, [B]open source contributions[/B], and browser security vulnerability analysis.

pwc.summary: Tech subsidiary of Samil PwC (Big4), handling digital transformation of accounting and consulting workflows. Led 3 projects, achieving [B]58.3% bundle reduction via CRA → Vite migration[/B], [B]building a custom CSP-compliant editor from scratch[/B], and single-handedly delivering Document AI with a custom PDF Viewer ([B]67% faster initial render[/B]) and Docker optimization ([B]91.36% image size reduction[/B]).

allra.title: Allra
allra.subtitle: Allra Fintech
allra.description: Pre-settlement financial service development for e-commerce sellers

details.text: Configured parallel test environment with [B]Dynamic metric[/B] and [B]Playwright Sharding[/B], reducing average test time by [B]67.7%[/B] ([L:https://team.allrafintech.co.kr/frontend-playwright-e2e-test-ci-optimization-d94b082509d8]blog[/L])
Replaced [B]ESLint + Prettier[/B] lint pipeline with [B]Biome[/B], reducing average CI lint time by [B]98%[/B]

Led [B]Datadog → OpenReplay[/B] cost-saving migration. Debugged Next.js group route sourcemap upload errors, configured CI/CD pipeline, and discovered missing handleError during source analysis, contributing via [L:https://github.com/openreplay/openreplay/issues/4476]open source PR[/L]
Unified all page OG/Twitter metadata into shared utils, introduced [B]Schema.org JSON-LD structured data[/B] (Organization, FAQ, Article)
Analyzed Chrome CPP source to identify autocomplete ignore cause, resolved with [B]Honeypot decoy field approach to bypass autofill[/B]
Refactored pre-settlement request logic with [B]TypeScript Discriminated Union[/B], enforcing impossible states at type level to prevent runtime errors and improve readability
[B]Server Action version skew errors[/B] and queue execution limits [L:/posts/2026-01-29]analyzed and documented[/L], [B]proposing tRPC migration that initiated team-wide adoption[/B]
Existing editor incompatible with in-house [B]CSP policy[/B] → migrated to react-quill-new and built a custom image resize module to resolve security violations

details.text: [B]Designed domain-separated MSW handlers for 6+ domains[/B] (auth, user, contract, payment, withdrawal, site management)

Implemented middleware auth guard with [B]iron-session[/B] encrypted cookies, route-level access control via menu permission mapping, and client-side [B]AuthGuard[/B] to prevent unauthenticated UI flash
Applied [B]KST timezone correction[/B] to notice and site management date fields after identifying the server's Seoul timezone assumption (hotfix)
Removed [B]deprecated X-Amz-ACL header[/B] from S3 presigned PUT requests, resolving upload 403 errors
